nodes.label: impeccable/
observations: Root directory of the impeccables UI craft skill
Contains agents/, scripts/, reference/ subdirectories
Skill descriptor at SKILL.md registers 22 commands across Build, Evaluate, Refine, Enhance, Fix, Iterate categories

nodes.label: SKILL.md
observations: Skill descriptor and routing hub for the impeccable skill
Total lines: 168, non-empty lines: 146

Defines 22 sub-commands: craft, shape, teach, document, extract, critique, audit, polish, bolder, quieter, distill, harden, onboard, animate, colorize, typeset, layout, delight, overdrive, clarify, adapt, optimize, live
Shares 8 design laws: color (OKLCH), theme scene sentence, typography 65-75ch, layout, motion bans, absolute bans (side-stripe borders, gradient text, glassmorphism), AI slop test
References registers: brand.md and product.md for register-specific rules
Houses shared design laws including color strategy, theme, typography, layout, motion, and absolute bans

nodes.label: impeccable-asset-producer.md
observations: Agent: Asset production for impeccability craft
Total lines: 101, agents/impeccable-asset-producer.md
Goal: production cleanup of raster assets from approved mocks, no new art direction
Core rule: preserve visual role, silhouette, palette, lighting, material, texture, camera angle
Input contract: approved mock path, crops, output directory, dimensions, format, transparency needs
Three buckets: produce (needs generation), direct (can ship as crop), semantic (HTML/CSS/SVG)
Output: manifest grouped by produce/direct/semantic with qa_status
Uses imagegen skill for production, prefers true alpha transparency
Contrasts: removes UI text, nav, buttons; preserves only physical marks if parent specifies

nodes.label: impeccable-paths.mjs
observations: Script: Path resolution utilities for impeccability
Total lines: 110, scripts/impeccable-paths.mjs
Node.js ESM module with exports
Exports constants: IMPECCABLE_DIR, LIVE_DIR, CRITIQUE_DIR
Provides path helpers for: design sidecar, live config, live server info, sessions, annotations, critique dirs
Supports legacy fallback paths (.impeccable-live/)
Used by live.mjs and other scripts for consistent directory resolution

nodes.label: live-accept.mjs
observations: Script: Variant accept/discard CLI for live sessions
Total lines: 595, scripts/live-accept.mjs
Node.js ESM module with auto-execute on direct run
Two modes: --discard removes variants and restores original; --variant N accepts one variant
Handles HTML, JSX, TSX, Vue, Svelte, Astro file extensions
Deep div-depth tracking for accurate JSX wrapper expansion (expandReplaceRange)
Carbonize support: preserves colocated <style> blocks with markers for background CSS integration
Exports: findMarkerBlock, extractOriginal, extractVariant, extractCss, deindentContent, detectCommentSyntax
Falls back to agent-driven flow if session markers live in a generated file
Imports is-generated.mjs for generated file detection

nodes.label: live-wrap.mjs
observations: Script: Element wrapping CLI for live variant sessions
Total lines: 632, scripts/live-wrap.mjs
Node.js ESM module with auto-execute on direct run
Searches project files by query (ID, class combo, tag, text snippet) to find elements
Wraps found element with variant scaffolding (original + variant slots)
Generates different wrapper syntax for HTML vs JSX/TSX files
Ambiguity detection: when multiple elements match, filters by textContent
Detects comment syntax (HTML vs JSX) and style mode (scoped vs Astro global)
Provides CSS authoring guidance in output (scope rules or global-prefixed selectors)
Exports: buildSearchQueries, findElement, findClosingLine, detectCommentSyntax

nodes.label: live.md
observations: Reference: Live variant mode workflow
Total lines: 622, non-empty: 413, 37 sections
Comprehensive live-mode protocol: contract, start, poll loop, recovery
Covers generate/fallback/accept/discard/prefetch/exit/cleanup flows
Includes first-time setup, drift-heal warning, CSP detection, consent prompt template
Troubleshooting section for common issues
Core reference for live-accept.mjs and live-wrap.mjs scripts

nodes.label: motion-design.md
observations: Reference: Motion design principles
Total lines: 109, non-empty: 73, 8 sections
Covers duration 100/300/500 rule, easing curves, premium motion materials
Staggered animations, reduced motion, perceived performance, rendering performance
Key resource for animate and overdrive commands

nodes.label: onboard.md
observations: Reference: Onboarding design patterns
Total lines: 234, non-empty: 179, 22 sections
Covers onboarding needs assessment, principles (show don't tell, make optional, time to value)
Designs for: initial product onboarding, feature discovery, guided tours, interactive tutorials
Documentation/help patterns, empty state design, implementation patterns, verification
Reference for onboard command

nodes.label: optimize.md
observations: Reference: UI performance optimization
Total lines: 258, non-empty: 211, 13 sections
Covers performance assessment, loading performance, rendering performance
Animation performance, React/framework optimization, network optimization
Core Web Vitals: LCP < 2.5s, FID < 100ms, CLS < 0.1
Performance monitoring and verification strategies
Reference for optimize command

nodes.label: overdrive.md
observations: Reference: Extraordinary visual effects
Total lines: 130, non-empty: 90, 20 sections
Approach: propose before building, iterate with browser automation
Assess extraordinary per surface: visual/marketing, functional UI, performance-critical, data-heavy
Toolkit: cinematic transitions, scroll-linked animation, canvas beyond CSS, data animation
Implement with discipline: progressive enhancement, performance rules
Reference for overdrive command

nodes.label: personas.md
observations: Reference: Persona-based design testing
Total lines: 179, non-empty: 133, 8 sections
Five personas: Impatient Power User (Alex), Confused First-Timer (Jordan)
Accessibility-Dependent User (Sam), Deliberate Stress Tester (Riley), Distracted Mobile User (Casey)
Guidance on selecting personas and creating project-specific personas
Used during critique and audit commands

nodes.label: polish.md
observations: Reference: Final quality polish pass
Total lines: 242, non-empty: 184, 19 sections
Covers design system discovery, pre-polish assessment
12 polish dimensions: alignment, IA, typography, color/contrast, interaction states
Micro-interactions, copy, icons/images, forms, edge cases, responsiveness, performance, code quality
Polish checklist for systematic verification
Reference for polish command

nodes.label: product.md
observations: Reference: Product register rules
Total lines: 62, non-empty: 41, 9 sections
Defines product register (app UI, dashboard, tool: design SERVES the product)
Product slop test, typography, color, layout, components, motion
Product bans (on top of shared absolute bans) and permissions
Loaded by SKILL.md when register is identified as product

nodes.label: quieter.md
observations: Reference: Tone down overstimulating designs
Total lines: 99, non-empty: 72, 10 sections
Register, assess current state, plan refinement
Refine through: color, visual weight reduction, simplification, motion reduction, composition
Verification quality checklist
Reference for quieter command

nodes.label: responsive-design.md
observations: Reference: Responsive design patterns
Total lines: 114, non-empty: 83, 10 sections
Mobile-first approach, content-driven breakpoints
Input method detection beyond screen size, safe areas/notch handling
Responsive images with srcset width descriptors and picture element art direction
Layout adaptation patterns, real device testing guidance
Reference for adapt command

nodes.label: shape.md
observations: Reference: UX/UI planning workflow
Total lines: 165, non-empty: 106, 15 sections
Philosophy, Phase 1 discovery interview with interview cadence
Interview covers: purpose/context, content/data, design direction, scope, constraints, anti-goals
Phase 1.5: visual direction probe (capability-gated)
Phase 2: design brief structure
Reference for shape command

nodes.label: spatial-design.md
observations: Reference: Spatial design principles
Total lines: 100, non-empty: 66, 14 sections
Spacing systems: 4pt base instead of 8pt, semantic token naming
Grid systems: self-adjusting grid, visual hierarchy via squint test
Cards are not required, container queries, optical adjustments
Touch targets vs visual size, depth and elevation
Reference for layout command

nodes.label: teach.md
observations: Reference: Context setup workflow
Total lines: 156, non-empty: 100, 13 sections
Six-step flow: load current state, explore codebase, ask strategic questions
Interview mode (not confirmation mode), minimum viable interview
Covers register, users/purpose, brand/personality, accessibility/inclusion
Write PRODUCT.md, decide on DESIGN.md, confirm and wrap up
Reference for teach command

nodes.label: typeset.md
observations: Reference: Typography refinement workflow
Total lines: 124, non-empty: 87, 11 sections
Register, assess current typography, plan improvements
Systematic improvement: font selection, hierarchy, readability, details, weight consistency
Verification and live-mode signature params
Reference for typeset command

nodes.label: typography.md
observations: Reference: Typography system reference
Total lines: 159, non-empty: 102, 15 sections
Classic principles: vertical rhythm, modular scale and hierarchy, readability and measure
Font selection and pairing principles, web font loading strategies
Modern web typography: fluid type, OpenType features, rendering polish
Typography system architecture, accessibility considerations
Supporting reference for typeset command

nodes.label: ux-writing.md
observations: Reference: UX writing guidance
Total lines: 107, non-empty: 69, 16 sections
Button label problem, error message formula and templates
Don't blame the user, empty states as opportunities
Voice vs tone, writing for accessibility, writing for translation (expansion planning)
Consistency terminology problem, avoid redundant copy, loading states, confirmation dialogs
Form instructions guidance
Reference for clarify command

note_on_discrepancies: 12 of 16 batch-8 files do not exist on disk. The extraction at ua-file-extract-results-8.json instead captured 16 reference/*.md files from impeccable/reference/ (different set). Nodes above combine: (a) reference docs from the extraction file, (b) the 3 script + 1 agent + SKILL.md that exist on disk.
